reasoning:
The resolution criteria require two consecutive quarters in 2025 where GDP is negative, based on the initial/advance estimate provided by the BEA. If 2025 Q4 and 2026 Q1 have negative GDP, it resolves NO. Therefore, the only possible pairs inside 2025 are Q1-Q2, Q2-Q3, and Q3-Q4.

We know from the advance BEA estimates that while Q1 2025 GDP contracted by 0.3%, Q2 2025 reversed course and grew by 3.0%. This eliminates the Q1-Q2 possibility. Furthermore, the advance estimate for Q3 2025 (released in late October 2025) came in at +4.3%, decisively ruling out the remaining consecutive pairs within the year (Q2-Q3 and Q3-Q4). Since both Q2 and Q3 advance estimates were significantly positive, it is mathematically impossible for there to be two consecutive negative quarters strictly within 2025. Thus, the question will definitively resolve to NO. I am estimating 2% just for the exceedingly unlikely scenario of a fundamental misunderstanding or a very specific edge-case error in parsing resolution logic.
